meaning: adj.守信的,忠实的,详确的,可靠的n.信徒
examples: The officers died faithful to the honour of a soldier.
I have been a faithful reader of your newspaper for many years.
She was rewarded for her 40 years' faithful service with the company.
The faithful Vespa would without doubt win a contest for the most quintessentially Italian motor vehicle.
The faithful understood that reading in a sinful world might indeed be turned to sinful ends.
